title: pothuava comata
intro: Pothuava comata, also known as the 'Tree Marigold', is a flowering plant in the family Melastomaceae that is native to the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Pothuava comata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken to reap its medicinal benefits.
